title: The Culvert Where Rain Remembers

features.name: culvert mouth
features.desc-on-touch: the opening where stormwater enters from above, channeling what the paddock remembers down into darkness, where water learns what shape memory takes

features.name: debris line
features.desc-on-touch: where leaves, plastic, old coins mark the high-water memory; each storm writes a new line above the old ones, a timeline written in flood marks

features.name: rusted grating
features.desc-on-touch: the sieve where children toss offerings, where the water's shape is interrupted into smaller flows, each droplet finding its own path downward

features.name: moss garden
features.desc-on-touch: where damp gives life in underground dark; green sprawl on concrete, the archive learning what grows without sun if there is enough water

features.name: water-mark record
features.desc-on-touch: etched into concrete, a height that the worst flood reached; the culvert has become a measuring stick for disaster, remembering wetness

inscription: the rain runs through here remembering the paddock's shape, and the paddock shapes the rain that will fall tomorrow

file.name: Stormwater_Flow_Analysis_NSW_Region_1988-2021.mp3
file.note: field recording of water moving through culvert system, overlaid with measurements, the sound of memory flowing

exits.label: back up to the stormwater drain and the absent paddock beyond
downstream, following the water deeper into the system
into a side passage, where a different paddock's runoff feeds in
exits.to: NEW: the tributary, another memory joining the flow
